selection_criteria.group_a: Pre-consensus dislocated quality: quality+moat gates passed, decline not FUNDAMENTAL_CONCERN, >=18% below 52wH, conviction >=55, ranked by conviction. Top 5. Holdings excluded.
selection_criteria.group_b: Quality at entry window: conviction >=60, RSI < 55, not in Group A. Top 7. Holdings excluded.
selection_criteria.scores: conviction_score comes from generate-conviction-ranking.cjs (scanner gates + signal tiers), not hand-typed values.

AVGO.name: Broadcom
AVGO.selection_reason: Track A: gates passed (quality, valuation, dislocation, moat) · conviction 81 · -20.7% from 52wH, RSI 51.9 · decline read: Sentiment disconnect
AVGO.early_entry_signal: Revenue growing 23.9% while stock is 20.7% below its high — the fundamentals and price are moving in opposite directions. This is the setup. Revenue +23.9% YoY (solid growth) · Gross margin 67.8% — platform-level pricing power · FCF $26914M (42.1% FCF margin) — strong cash generation
AVGO.moat_summary: Deep hyperscaler relationships. Custom ASIC design is irreplaceable once embedded — hyperscalers design multi-year chip roadmaps around AVGO.
AVGO.next_catalyst: Q3 FY2026: custom ASIC pipeline + AI revenue guide exceeding $200% growth.
AVGO.invalidation: Hyperscaler AI capex decelerates >20%. Infrastructure software segment deteriorates further.

KLAC.early_entry_signal: Revenue growing 23.9% while stock is 28.8% below its high — the fundamentals and price are moving in opposite directions. This is the setup. Revenue +23.9% YoY (solid growth) · FCF $3747M (30.8% FCF margin) — strong cash generation · P/S 2.4× — cheap by any standard
KLAC.moat_summary: Level-2 supply chain — underappreciated, 2-4 competitors, high switching costs

BWXT.name: BWX Technologies
BWXT.selection_reason: Track A: gates passed (quality, valuation, dislocation, moat) · conviction 77 · -26.9% from 52wH, RSI 40.8 · decline read: Sharp recent drop — investigate
BWXT.early_entry_signal: Market prices BWXT as a cyclical industrial compounder. Correct read: legally mandated defense utility with 2.3x annual revenue in contracted backlog already signed. The $8.65B is not speculative — it is executed contracts. SMR upside is free optionality on top.
BWXT.moat_summary: Legal monopoly. Federal law requires US Navy nuclear propulsion components from BWXT — no substitute, no foreign alternative permitted. Zero AI disruption risk.
BWXT.next_catalyst: Additional naval nuclear contract awards throughout 2026. Q2 and Q3 2026 earnings confirming 60%+ backlog-to-revenue conversion.
BWXT.invalidation: Defense budget cut >20% to naval nuclear programs. Quarterly revenue falls below $870M for two consecutive quarters.

evidence.claim: $8.65B backlog as of Mar 31 2026; Government Operations backlog ~$7.0B, +93% YoY incl. $1.4B naval pricing agreement
Note: backlog/revenue is ~2.5x on annualized Q1 revenue, not the 2.3x stated in the thesis; 'legal monopoly' = sole-source naval contractor in practice, not statute

KNSL.name: Kinsale Capital
KNSL.selection_reason: Track A: gates passed (quality, valuation, dislocation, moat) · conviction 77 · -35.2% from 52wH, RSI 50.6 · decline read: Sentiment disconnect
KNSL.early_entry_signal: Nobody talks about insurance in an AI bull market. KNSL is down 24% with no deterioration in underwriting quality — the dislocation is narrative, not data. This is the Buffett entry template: boring business, down for sentiment reasons, best-in-class economics compounding at 15%+ for a decade, high FCF, zero AI disruption risk.
KNSL.moat_summary: Proprietary actuarial data and technology-first underwriting culture built over 15+ years — 77.4% combined ratio cannot be replicated by a new entrant. The moat compounds every year the database grows.
KNSL.next_catalyst: Q2 2026 earnings: combined ratio confirmation below 80% proves underwriting quality intact.
KNSL.invalidation: Combined ratio rises above 85% for two consecutive quarters. Premium growth falls below 0% YoY.

CLS.name: Celestica
CLS.selection_reason: Track B window: conviction 74 (tier B) · RSI 46.3 inside entry window · -29.4% from 52wH
CLS.early_entry_signal: Druckenmiller-style early entry: same AI infrastructure demand as AVGO, but no major fund showed significant new CLS position in Q1 2026 13F filings. +53% revenue growth with $19B FY guide raised — the crowd hasn't arrived. Trades at 18x forward PE while AVGO trades at 32x for the same demand tailwind. The mispricing exists because CLS is classified as a boring contract manufacturer, not an AI infrastructure name.
CLS.moat_summary: Deep hyperscaler manufacturing integration — multi-year supply agreements with design-to-manufacturing integration create switching costs. CLS is embedded in hardware design cycles, not just assembly.
CLS.next_catalyst: Q2 2026 earnings: FY guide raised further above $19B confirms AI hardware demand acceleration. Major analyst initiation at bulge-bracket bank would force institutional recognition.
CLS.invalidation: Hyperscaler AI capex cut >15%. Loss of a top-2 customer relationship. Revenue growth drops below 20% for two consecutive quarters.
